good: clear and intentional
tip: Specificity forces the AI to make design decisions instead of guessing

good: high-contrast and dynamic
tip: Name the visual property — contrast, motion, density

good: [select aesthetic keyword below]
tip: 'Modern' means nothing to an AI. Pick a named style.

good: visually striking and purposeful
tip: Every element must justify its existence

good: typographically strong with clear visual hierarchy
tip: Hierarchy = size+weight+spacing+color in that order

good: visually precise with intentional contrast ratio
tip: Beauty is subjective. Contrast ratio is measurable.

good: reduced to essential elements only
tip: Minimalism is a decision, not a default

good: uncluttered with 8pt grid spacing and intentional whitespace
tip: Name the spacing system — 4pt, 8pt, 12pt

good: polished, authoritative, and on-brand
tip: On-brand = specific color, type, motion tokens

good: frictionless with intuitive affordances and 44px touch targets
tip: Touch targets are measurable. Frictionless is a UX goal.

good: fluid grid at 375/768/1280/1920px breakpoints with touch target compliance
tip: Name the breakpoints. Responsive without breakpoints is guesswork.

good: LCP<2.5s, CLS<0.1, FID<100ms, initial JS<200kb
tip: Web Vitals are the standard — use them directly

good: increase contrast ratio and focal-point visual weight
tip: 'Pop' = contrast + proximity + size. Define which.

good: define trigger, duration, easing, and purpose first
tip: Animation without purpose = distraction

good: remove every element that doesn't serve a function
tip: Minimalism is ruthless subtraction, not sparse decoration

good: redesign contrast, color semantics, and elevation for dark-native
tip: Inverting light mode ≠ dark mode. Elevation logic changes.

good: mobile-first: design at 375px viewport before scaling up
tip: Mobile-friendly = afterthought. Mobile-first = constraint-driven design.

good: apply 8pt baseline grid with intentional visual rhythm
tip: Random whitespace looks accidental. Grid-based spacing looks designed.

good: WCAG AA: 4.5:1 text contrast, 3:1 UI contrast, keyboard-navigable, aria-labels on all interactive elements
tip: Accessibility is measurable. Name the standard.

good: cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1) 320ms spring easing with controlled overshoot
tip: Spring easing feels physical. Ease-in-out feels digital.

good: backdrop-filter:blur(20px), background:rgba(255,255,255,0.06), border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12)
tip: Specify all three properties — blur, fill opacity, border opacity

good: modular scale 1.25, line-height 1.6 body/1.1 display, tabular-nums for data, clamp() for fluid sizing
tip: Modular scale creates harmony. clamp() removes media query bloat.

good: hover 150ms ease-out, press 80ms scale(0.97), focus-visible 2px ring offset-2
tip: Spec all three states. Consistency = quality signal.

good: skeleton shimmer matching exact content shape, 400ms shimmer loop, fade-in on content arrival
tip: Skeleton must mirror real content layout — not generic rectangles

good: hook (problem) → agitation → solution → proof → CTA. One idea per sentence.
tip: PAS framework converts better than feature lists

good: semantic HTML landmarks, schema.org JSON-LD, OG tags, canonical URLs, LCP image preloaded
tip: SEO is technical. Name every attribute.

good: typed errors, retry with exponential backoff, fallback UI, error boundary, toast notification with action
tip: Error handling has 5 layers. Name which ones you want.

good: stateless functions, horizontal scaling, read replicas for DB, CDN for assets, queue for async jobs
tip: Scalability is architecture. Name the pattern.

good: input validation (zod), parameterized queries (no SQL injection), CORS policy, rate limiting, JWT rotation
tip: Security is a checklist. Give the AI the checklist.

good: define event taxonomy: event_name, properties:{}, user_id, timestamp — then implement
tip: Taxonomy first, SDK second. Retrofitting analytics is 10x harder.

good: instrument: page_view, session_start, [feature]_clicked, [goal]_completed, error_occurred
tip: 5 events well-tracked beats 50 events inconsistently tracked.

good: line chart for trends over time, bar for comparisons, scatter for correlations, funnel for conversion
tip: Chart type = data relationship. Pick the relationship, chart follows.

good: KPI cards (delta + sparkline) + trend chart + data table + filter panel + date range picker
tip: Every dashboard needs these 5. If it's missing one, it's incomplete.

good: semantic HTML landmarks, title+meta+OG per page, JSON-LD schema, canonical URL, LCP image preloaded with fetchpriority=high
tip: SEO is 5 specific technical attributes. Name them all.

good: primary keyword in H1 + first 100 words + URL slug + meta description. Secondary keywords in H2s. Internal links to 3 related pages.
tip: On-page SEO is a formula. Apply the formula, not the vibe.

good: define: model, system prompt, tools (name+schema+execute), memory type, max_steps, output format
tip: An agent without defined tools is just a chatbot. Tools are what make it an agent.

good: trigger (event/schedule/webhook) → validate input → transform data → call API → handle errors → log result → notify on failure
tip: Automation has 7 required stages. Missing any one causes silent failures.

good: define: trigger source, data schema in, LLM call (model+prompt+output schema), tools available, error fallback, output destination
tip: LLM call is one step in the workflow, not the whole workflow.

good: add index on columns in WHERE/JOIN/ORDER BY clauses, use EXPLAIN ANALYZE, add connection pooling, cache hot queries in Redis
tip: Indexes solve 80% of DB performance issues. Run EXPLAIN first.

good: profile first: measure LCP, TTFB, JS execution time, DB query time — then optimize the bottleneck only
tip: Optimize the slowest thing, not everything. Profile before you optimize.

good: cache-aside for DB reads (Redis, 5min TTL), CDN for static assets (1yr cache-control), stale-while-revalidate for API responses
tip: Three separate cache layers. Each solves a different latency problem.

good: unknown type with type narrowing, or define the exact interface
tip: 'unknown' forces type checking. 'any' disables it. Never use 'any' in production.

good: type guard function with isinstance check — never use 'as' without validation
tip: 'as' is a lie to the compiler. Runtime errors follow. Use type guards.

good: explicit null checks with type narrowing — know when data can be null
tip: Optional chaining hides bugs. Explicit checks surface them early.

good: write unit test: arrange input, act, assert output matches expectation
tip: Manual testing doesn't scale. The test is the documentation.

good: test the behavior — what does the function do, not how it does it
tip: Testing implementation makes refactoring impossible. Test behavior only.

good: test with real objects where possible; mock only external I/O (APIs, DBs)
tip: Over-mocking creates tests that pass when production fails.

good: mobile-first: design at 375px, then layer up with min-width media queries
tip: Mobile-first forces constraint-driven design. Desktop-first creates retrofits.

good: pointer events API — pointerdown/pointerup handles mouse + touch + stylus
tip: Touch events don't fire on desktop. Pointer events work everywhere.

good: pointer event + 44px minimum touch target + 8px gap between targets
tip: 44px touch target is Apple HIG + WCAG requirement. Below it = failed accessibility.

good: define: HTTP method + path + request schema + response schema + error codes
tip: An API without a schema is a guess. Define the contract before building.

good: HTTP 201 for created, 400 for validation, 401 for auth, 404 for missing, 422 for unprocessable, 500 for server error
tip: Correct status codes enable client-side error handling without parsing response bodies.

good: Authorization: Bearer token in header — never credentials in URL (they log)
tip: URLs are logged everywhere. Headers are not. API keys in URLs leak to server logs, browser history, proxies.

good: useEffect for sync only — prefer useMemo, useCallback, derived state for transforms
tip: 90% of useEffect usages are wrong. If you're deriving state, use useMemo. If you're handling events, use callbacks.

good: React Context for theme/auth, Zustand for cross-component state, URL params for navigation state
tip: Context for slow-changing global data. Zustand for fast-changing UI state. Not everything needs a store.

good: key={item.id} — unique stable identifier from the data, never array index
tip: Index keys break animations, form state, and reconciliation. Always use a stable ID.

good: rem for text (respects user font preferences), px for borders/shadows, % for layout
tip: px ignores user accessibility settings. rem inherits from root. Mix intentionally.

good: CSS Grid or Flexbox for layout — absolute only for overlays, tooltips, and decorative elements
tip: Absolute positioning breaks reflow. Grid/Flex adapts to content. Use absolute sparingly.
